title: erodium petraeum subsp. glandulosum
intro: Erodium petraeum subsp. glandulosum, also known as the glandular alpine geranium, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the geranium family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and it is believed to possess antioxidant and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Erodium petraeum subsp. glandulosum are said to include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its extracts are sometimes added to creams and ointments for topical application.
